commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content:
Beasts. Racesh denotes horses of extraordinary swiftness, (Bo chart) or dromedaries Junius translates, "post-horses. "
King: so also the Septuagint. Protestants, "the officers were, every man according to his charge. "The twelve governors employed others to bring all necessary provisions, (Haydock) to the places where the king was travelling; (Calmet) or they took care not only of the king's table, but they had also the general inspection over his stables. (Haydock)
Few oats are grown in the East. They feed their horses on barley and straw. (Calmet)

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
